narrative.context: When you mention a mutual friend, your friend quickly changes the topic and fidgets. You’re unsure what they’re feeling.
narrative.prompt: What helps you perceive their emotion accurately?

options.label: Notice avoidance cues and check gently
options.description: Observe topic shift + fidgeting, then ask privately: 'Did something happen? We can talk if you want.'

ei.rationale: Uses behavioral cues to infer possible discomfort while seeking confirmation—accurate perception.

options.label: Assume they’re busy and move on

ei.rationale: Avoids pressure, but may miss emotional information; perception remains untested.

options.label: Assume they’re gossiping
options.description: Conclude they are hiding something and become suspicious.

ei.rationale: Jumps to a negative interpretation without evidence; perception becomes biased.

options.label: Force the topic
options.description: Insist they explain immediately in front of others.

ei.rationale: Increases social threat and reduces accurate emotional reading.
